g.text: twisting secondary passage — curves designed to disorient
larger chambers — broad shelving grooves in walls
deep passage — metal traces (gold & copper) embedded in walls
Italic labels = assumptions based on survey, not confirmed purpose
· Passages twist and curve throughout — possibly designed to disorient invaders
· Several chambers contain murals of unknown animals · Gold and copper metal embedded in deep passage walls (purpose unknown)
· Oddly-shaped chamber groupings in deeper areas do not follow standard architectural patterns
· Section 15 boundary and room estimates are projected from Section 14 layout — actual layout may differ significantly
By order of Highprince Hatham, in service to the Coalition of Monarchs at Urithiru
Let it be known that the bearer of this writ is granted full authority to enter, survey, and explore
the uncharted areas designated as Section 15 of Level 3, Southern Arc, including all chambers,
passages, and balcony structures therein, up to and including those areas where the tower
structure melds into the mountain rock.
The bearer and their company are authorized to clear obstructions, mark passages, and secure
chambers for future habitation. Any functioning fabrials, artifacts, or items of scholarly interest
are to be catalogued and reported to the surveyors' office. Nothing is to be removed without
express approval from the Highprince's steward. Furthermore, the bearer is granted authority to
detain any unauthorized persons found within Section 15 and escort them to the nearest guard post
